widgetData.source_sentence: he did not give us the promised jobs and is not returning the money either. When we asked for our money back, he threatened us with false allegations and threats to our lives, and his father is a lawyer in the civil court and is a very aggressive mafia-type person who is misusing his power against us. There was an agreement at the police station between us that he would pay Rs. 30,230 every month, but even after four months have passed, he has not paid a single rupee. Therefore, we request Mr. Sir to kindly file a case against them under the relevant section and conduct legal proceedings. Thank you. widgetData.source_sentence: First Information Report: Seizure of counterfeit goods—seized 20 liters of raw alcohol and arrest under FIR Abhi0 Section 60 Ex Act at Shah0 Sadar Bazaar Police Station on date 17.3.24. Today, SI Pradeep Kumar and HC 433 Sanjiv Kumar were on duty for surveillance in Shanti Vyas area when we reached Avantii Bai Lodhi Park Chinour after checking and patrolling. A person came towards us with a plastic jar in hand containing some substance. Seeing us, he panicked and tried to run away. Upon being stopped and asked to halt, he did not comply. We chased him and caught him near Avantii Bai Lodhi Park, about 50 steps towards Chinour around 5:50 PM. The arrested person was questioned for his name and address, and a search was conducted. He identified himself as Kali Charan, son of Nanhu, residing in Chinour, Sadar Bazaar Police Station, district Shahjahanpur. During the search, a plastic jar containing approximately 20 liters of substance was found in his hand. Opening the lid of the jar, we

widgetData.source_sentence: and requested forgiveness, stating that he had purchased the firearm and cartridge from an unknown person several years ago for Rs. 3000. He mentioned that he had been to jail three times and kept it for his safety. This offense is punishable under Section 325 of the Arms Act. At around 04:35, he was informed of his offense under the aforementioned section and taken into custody. During the arrest, orders and instructions of the Supreme Court and Human Rights Commission were strictly followed. Due to the late hour, no witnesses could be found among the public. The detainee's family will be informed through appropriate means after reaching the station. The seized 315 bore firearm and one live cartridge 315 bore were wrapped in white cloth, sealed, and stamped as evidence. The accused was read and heard the report written by me, Sub Inspector Navrang Sonkar, using a torchlight and car light, and all relevant parties signed. The seized firearm and cartridge can also be used to kill

widgetData.source_sentence: aged 35 years, who is a wanted criminal with a reward of Rs. 25,000 announced by the local police station. He is registered under Section 132/20, Section 2/3(1) of the UP Gangster Act at the Haldi Police Station, Balia district. A search revealed a .315 caliber firearm and a live cartridge in his possession. The seized firearm has an 8 cm steel barrel, a 9.5 cm steel stock with a hammer, trigger, and trigger guard, and a spring-loaded catch mechanism. The bullet is made of brass and lead. The items were placed in a transparent plastic box and sealed with a sample stamp. The accused was informed of his crime and taken into custody around 9:05 PM. During the arrest and seizure, all witnesses were present and gave statements. Human rights were observed during the arrest and seizure, and the accused's family will be notified of the arrest. The report was read out and signed by all concerned, including Prabhakar Shukla, Mayank Yadav, and others. A copy of the FIR was given to the accused,
sentences: Who is the petitioner in the First Information Report and what is her relationship to the victim?

widgetData.source_sentence: (First Information Report): A plastic bottle containing approximately 10 liters of raw illegal liquor and an arrest under Section 60(1) Ex Act, Thana Asandra district Barabanki on today's date 24/01/2024, I, H/O Amar Bahadur Singh, along with H/O Ramji Yadav and H/O Bharat Sharma, departed from the police station towards the highway barrier for peacekeeping and inspection in the area. The special informant came and informed that a person from Devra Jhalia village was coming towards the new road carrying a bottle with raw illegal liquor intended for sale, and if we acted quickly, he could be caught. Believing the informant's information, we proceeded towards the address with the informant. Just before reaching Devra Jhalia village, the informant pointed out that the person coming was the one they were looking for and then left. As soon as we, the police, turned towards the village, the individual noticed us and became suspicious, trying to flee but was surrounded and caught at the Devra
sentences: Which organizations' orders and instructions were followed during the arrest?
On what date did the incident involving the arrest under Section 60(1) Ex Act take place?
